hero.headline: Round 9 visual operating language
hero.subheadline: Professional DB table cards, readable topology and role-aware visual director controls for massive ERP/MES/eQMS schema graphs.
hero.promise: Canvas now behaves like an enterprise control plane instead of a raw ERD wall.

cardModes.label: Architect mode
cardModes.detail: PK/FK, object type, index density and schema topology stay visible while background noise collapses.

cardModes.label: Compliance mode
cardModes.detail: RLS, policies, approvals and evidence cues surface directly on the card without opening inspector first.

cardModes.label: Manufacturing mode
cardModes.detail: Traceability, execution and quality semantics become primary, helping planners and MES engineers read flows fast.

cardModes.label: Builder mode
cardModes.detail: Workflow, API and runtime binding signals are elevated for metadata admins and app builders.

edgeLenses.label: Cross-domain topology
edgeLenses.detail: Highlights cross-domain dependencies and suppresses local noise to expose architectural coupling.

edgeLenses.label: Governance lens
edgeLenses.detail: Brings approval, policy and security-relevant relations to the foreground.

edgeLenses.label: Traceability lens
edgeLenses.detail: Surfaces genealogy, lot/serial and quality lineage paths for manufacturing reviews.

edgeLenses.label: Runtime lens
edgeLenses.detail: Emphasizes workflow, API and builder-facing relationship signals.

laneGuides.label: Master data lane
laneGuides.detail: Identity-rich reference tables with heavy inbound dependency pressure.

laneGuides.label: Planning ERP lane
laneGuides.detail: Commercial, order and planning orchestration surfaces.

laneGuides.label: MES execution lane
laneGuides.detail: Dispatch, execution, shift, tool and operator tables rendered as one readable operating strip.

laneGuides.label: Traceability lane
laneGuides.detail: Lot, serial, genealogy and inventory edges stay legible under heavy density.

laneGuides.label: eQMS lane
laneGuides.detail: Deviation, CAPA, approval and evidence-heavy surfaces grouped for review boards.

laneGuides.label: Infrastructure lane
laneGuides.detail: Foundation services remain visible without overpowering domain cards.

quickActions.label: Quiet topology
quickActions.effect: Muting default edges and preserving only focus neighborhood brightness.

quickActions.label: Switch card mode
quickActions.effect: Re-weights semantic emphasis for architect, compliance, manufacturing or builder personas.

quickActions.label: Toggle lane radar
quickActions.effect: Displays dominant domain strips with table density and domain narrative.

quickActions.label: Focus edge lens
quickActions.effect: Filters topology into cross-domain, governance, traceability or runtime overlays.

quickActions.label: Accessibility review
quickActions.effect: Keeps contrast, focus and redundant semantic cues inside WCAG enterprise posture.

accessibility.contrast: Card surfaces, text and non-text cues are designed to stay at WCAG AA-friendly contrast bands.
accessibility.focus: Cards keep a visible keyboard focus ring and selected neighborhood glow.

beautySystem.surfaceGrammar: neutral layered surfaces with domain rail and restrained semantic accents
beautySystem.motion: short, non-distracting transitions for focus, hover and selection

reviewGuides: At zoomed-out levels, the graph should read as lanes first and tables second.
No table card should carry more than two persistent semantic badges.
Default topology should feel quiet until a table, lane or lens is selected.
Selected neighborhood must remain readable without opening inspector.
